$comment: Guardrails for the dependency-hygiene loop. Budgets are set ASSUMING SOMETHING SPINS IDLE OVERNIGHT on a cron — an unattended loop with no ceiling is how a single bad turn burns a month of tokens before anyone notices. These caps are the brake.

budget.perRunTokens_comment: Hard ceiling for a single triage run (one cron firing). Enough to read audit/outdated output, judge a handful of findings, and write state — not enough to spiral.
budget.dailyTokens_comment: Sum across all runs in a calendar day. The cron fires once at 06:00, but manual re-runs and retries share this pool. When it's gone, the loop stops until tomorrow.
budget.maxRetries_comment: Per finding. If a bump fails verification (npm test / lint / audit) this many times, stop retrying and route it to ./inbox/ for a human. Retrying forever is how mistakes survive long enough to compound.

policy.autoMerge_comment: NEVER true. PRs are opened for humans; the loop does not merge its own work.
policy.allowMajorBumps_comment: Major / breaking bumps are flagged only, never attempted unattended. See the Stop section of .claude/skills/dep-triage/SKILL.md.
policy.uncertainGoesTo_comment: Anything below full confidence becomes a file in ./inbox/, not a PR.
